problem_addressed: Scientific discovery is lengthy and costly, limiting the number of research ideas researchers can explore.
core_claim: Agent Laboratory is an autonomous LLM-based framework that can complete the entire research process from idea to results while reducing costs by 84%.
key_contributions: Introduction of Agent Laboratory framework with three-stage pipeline (literature review, experimentation, report writing)
Demonstration that human feedback at each stage significantly improves research quality
Finding that o1-preview generates best research outcomes with state-of-the-art performance
novelty: First framework capable of completing entire research process autonomously using specialized LLM agents while enabling human intervention at each stage.
method_summary: Agent Laboratory uses a pipeline of specialized LLM-driven agents to process human research ideas through literature review, experimentation, and report writing stages, producing code repositories and research reports.
key_innovation: Multi-stage pipeline of specialized LLM agents that autonomously conduct complete research workflows while maintaining human intervention capabilities at critical decision points.
assumptions: LLMs can perform complex research tasks including literature review, experimentation, and report writing
Human feedback at each stage can meaningfully improve research quality
The framework can generate state-of-the-art machine learning code
theoretical_grounding: None - relies on empirical evaluation through human assessments and performance comparisons with existing methods.
title: Agent Laboratory: Using LLM Agents as Research Assistants
